items.title: SC allows Aakash Educational Services to go ahead with rights issue
items.description: The Supreme Court has allowed Aakash Educational Services to proceed with its rights issue. This fundraising move was challenged by Byju's bankrupt parent company, Think & Learn. The court asked Think & Learn to subscribe to the shares by next Monday. Aakash Educational Services assured the court that Think & Learn's shareholding would be protected.

items.title: Asian Paints faces near-term headwinds as weak Q3 dampens sentiment
items.description: Asian Paints stock dropped after a weak December quarter. Softening demand, extended monsoon, and competition impacted sentiment. The company expects market share gains in waterproofing and home decor. Despite slower decorative volume growth, margins expanded due to lower raw material costs. Brokerages have trimmed earnings estimates. Cost savings and new products will support profitability as demand recovery remains gradual.

items.title: GIFT Nifty up 45 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: The Nifty saw a strong recovery on Tuesday, gaining almost 200 points from its low to close at 25,725. Analysts anticipate continued market strength with a positive outlook. India VIX fell, indicating reduced market fear. Foreign investors were net sellers, while domestic investors bought shares. The Rupee strengthened against the US dollar.

items.title: Stocks in news: Maruti Suzuki, Dabur, Info Edge, GR Infraprojects, Dilip Buildcon
items.description: Indian markets closed higher on expiry day, recovering from early weakness with selective buying. Maruti Suzuki unveiled its e VITARA electric SUV starting at Rs 10.99 lakh with a unique BaaS model. Dabur appointed Herjit S Bhalla to lead its India business, while GR Infraprojects secured a railway construction project worth Rs 668 crore.

items.title: What lies ahead for Indian IT as brokerages reevaluate growth prospects?
items.description: Brokerages Nomura and UBS assess the impact of AI on Indian IT stocks, with Nomura suggesting current sell-offs are front-loading pains and UBS highlighting the importance of adapting to AI. Both acknowledge transition challenges but see potential for stock floors due to cash flows and dividends. Adaptability to AI adoption and pricing model shifts will be crucial for future valuations.

items.title: These 10 banking stocks have an upside potential of up to 28% in 1 year, according to analysts
items.description: In the corrective phase of the past year-and-a-half, almost every sector has been mauled by the bears. But even in such times, banking stocks have shown relative outperformance. And if they can ride this phase out, they will possibly move up as the broader sentiment recovers. The first signs of this might already have come in the last couple of trading sessions. Check out Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, for price targets of over 4,000 listed stocks along with detailed company analysis focusing on five key components ,earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum, to generate standardised scores. SR+ Reports is a complimentary offering to ETPrime members.

items.title: Fixed-price ATM pacts are the new normal for banks
items.description: Indian banks are moving to fixed-price contracts for ATM management. This change replaces older transaction-based models. Banks seek cost stability and assured service quality. This shift aims for predictable expenses and better performance from vendors. Many new ATM and cash recycler contracts are being awarded under this fixed-fee structure. This ensures consistent service and improved customer convenience.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra, DCB Bank name new directors
items.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank has appointed Ramesh Iyer as an additional and independent director. This appointment is effective February 17, 2026. Dr. Ashok Gulati will retire as an independent director on March 5, 2026. DCB Bank has also approved Suhail Nathani's appointment as an independent director. This takes effect from February 18, 2026.

items.title: Muthoot Microfin looks to buy company offering loan against property
items.description: Muthoot Microfin is exploring inorganic growth by acquiring a majority stake in two non-banking financial companies. This move aims to expand its micro loan against property business. The company is diversifying its portfolio following recent RBI policy changes. Muthoot Microfin plans to significantly increase its secured lending. This strategy will reduce reliance on unsecured microfinance loans.

items.title: Infosys' $400 billion AI dream fails to arrest stock slide, but target prices go up to Rs 2,050
items.description: Infosys' AI roadmap, targeting a $300-400 billion market, failed to impress investors, causing shares to tumble despite a bullish outlook from brokerages. While the company highlighted AI as a structural tailwind, market concerns persist over the immediate deflationary impact on legacy revenues versus the long-term benefits.

items.title: Private credit: Bringing an investment approach into lending
items.description: Private credit blends the discipline of lending with the forward-looking mindset of investing. By offering bespoke, negotiated capital structures across business lifecycles, it provides flexible, growth-oriented financing. As Indian businesses evolve, private credit is emerging as a vital, permanent component of modern capital structures rather than an alternative.

items.title: Earnings boost: 10 smallcaps that clocked biggest profit growth of up to 353% in Q3
items.description: While market volatility persisted over the past few quarters in the broader market, mainly due to earnings and valuations mismatch, several smallcap companies delivered strong profit figures in the December quarter. Source: Motilal Oswal

items.title: Ola Electric shares rebound 5% after 11% fall in 4 sessions. What’s driving the surge?
items.description: Ola Electric Mobility shares rose after the Bombay High Court stayed an arrest warrant issued against CEO Bhavish Aggarwal, easing investor concerns following the stock’s recent four-session decline.

items.title: Dilip Buildcon shares rally 4% as lowest bidder for Rs 702 crore Gujarat flood control project
items.description: Dilip Buildcon shares: The company emerged as the lowest bidder for a Rs 702-crore flood-control embankment project in Gujarat, to be executed on an EPC basis over 24 months along the Narmada River in Bharuch district.

items.title: India’s AIF industry crosses Rs 15 lakh crore as domestic capital & liquidity reshape private markets
items.description: India's Alternative Investment Fund (AIF) industry has surpassed ₹15.05 lakh crore in commitments, driven by domestic capital now dominating over 55% of investments. The emergence of secondary markets as a significant liquidity tool, alongside consistent alpha generation over public markets, signals a maturing Indian private capital landscape.

items.title: Infosys shares in focus on AI-first framework to capture $400 bn services opportunity. What are brokerages saying?
items.description: Infosys shares are in focus after it unveiled Infosys Topaz, its AI-first framework, aimed at helping enterprises unlock AI value at scale. Analysts see an incremental $300–400 billion AI services opportunity by 2030. Motilal Oswal maintains a Buy rating, Morgan Stanley an Equal Weight rating, while Q3FY26 results show stable margins and 9% revenue growth.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty subdued as IT selloff deepens; metal, PSU bank stocks gain
items.description: Indian benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex opened higher for a third consecutive session, boosted by gains in metal and PSU stocks. However, a significant selloff in IT stocks capped the overall market rise. Kwality Wall's India and Tech Mahindra were among the top decliners on the Sensex.

items.title: Buy, Sell or Hold? Motilal Oswal maintains buy on Varun Beverages; Goldman Sachs initiates buy on LG India
items.description: Brokerages maintain a positive outlook on select large-cap stocks. Motilal Oswal sees growth for Varun Beverages from Africa expansion, while Morgan Stanley is optimistic on Titan Company due to improved earnings. Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on LG Electronics India with a Buy call, citing market leadership and premium positioning.

items.title: LIC's Rs 17.5 lakh crore portfolio goes against the wind: IT stocks in, banks out
items.description: LIC is making a contrarian bet on the IT sector, investing heavily in companies like TCS and HCL Technologies while reducing its exposure to banking giants such as SBI and HDFC Bank.

items.title: Netweb Tech shares jump 4% after launch of ‘Make in India’ AI supercomputing systems powered by NVIDIA
items.description: In a bold stride towards technological advancement, Netweb Technologies India witnessed a boom in shares following the introduction of their cutting-edge AI supercomputers. The formidable Tyrone Camarero GB200 and the agile Tyrone Camarero Spark are designed to offer immense AI capabilities, leveraging the prowess of NVIDIA technology in line with the 'Make in India' vision.

items.title: Eternal shares in spotlight as OpenAI partnership set to boost AI across Zomato, Blinkit
items.description: Food delivery giant Eternal is boosting its AI integration through an expanded partnership with OpenAI. This collaboration will enhance AI across its consumer platforms like Zomato and Blinkit, as well as internal systems and social impact initiatives. The company aims to leverage advanced AI for better customer and partner interactions, and to streamline operations, potentially impacting its stock performance.

items.title: Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on LG Electronics India with Buy rating. Check target price and upside scope
items.description: Shares of LG Electronics India are in focus after Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Buy rating and a Rs 1,750 target, implying 15% upside. The brokerage cited strong category leadership and premium positioning. Q3 net profit fell 61% YoY to Rs 89.7 crore, hurt by higher operating expenses, though shares have risen 13% in a month.

items.title: One97 Communications among 5 stocks flashing bullish signals, hinting at a possible uptrend
items.description: On February 17, five stocks in the Nifty200 pack appeared on the bullish scanner of "White Marubozu," according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. A White Marubozu is a bullish candlestick pattern in technical analysis. It occurs when the opening price is the lowest for a given period, and the closing price is the highest.Represented by a long white (or green) candlestick with no upper shadow and a small or nonexistent lower shadow, it indicates strong buying pressure throughout the session. Traders interpret it as a signal that buyers were in control from the opening to the closing, often using such patterns for informed decisions about future price movements in financial markets."

items.title: Adani Power among 5 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.description: On February 17, five stocks from the NSE large-cap universe were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan, based on data from StockEdge.com. This is considered an uptrend signal when the RSI value crosses above 50 from below. "RSI trending up" means that the Relative Strength Index (RSI) values are increasing, suggesting strengthening momentum in the price of the stock. This can indicate potential upward movement and may be used by traders to identify buying opportunities.

items.title: Positive Breakout: These 13 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
items.description: In the Nifty500 pack, 13 stocks' closing prices crossed above their 200 DMA (Daily Moving Averages) on February 17, 2026, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. The 200-day daily moving average (DMA) is used by traders as a key indicator for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily timeframe, it is generally considered to be in an overall uptrend. Take a look:

items.title: Nvidia effect! E2E Networks, Netweb Tech shares soar up to 20% after collaboration with chip giant
items.description: Shares of E2E Networks and Netweb Technologies saw significant gains today. Both companies announced collaborations with chip giant Nvidia. E2E Networks is building an Nvidia GPU cluster for sovereign AI development. Netweb Technologies unveiled advanced AI infrastructure, including a 'Make in India' AI supercomputer. These developments highlight a strong focus on advancing artificial intelligence capabilities in India.

items.title: Small-cap stocks: Riskier, without a doubt. But time to be contrarian? 6 small-caps from different sectors to keep an eye on
items.description: Why look at small-caps when there are many large-cap stocks available at decent valuations? But then, a counter-question: When is it a good time to buy small-cap stocks? When they are rising every second day? Or when there is a correction and the commentary is all negative? The answer is obvious. In early 2024 or before that, in 2023, you could pick almost any small-cap stock and be a winner. That will not happen any more. So, avoid “narrative stocks” and stay away from those with high valuations without matching earnings growth. But that does not mean you should avoid all small-cap stocks. It is probably time to look at them rationally.

items.title: ONGC, Oil India, other upstream oil stocks fall up to 4%. What’s triggering the decline?
items.description: Upstream oil stocks, including ONGC, Oil India and Seamec, fell sharply on Wednesday as crude prices weakened amid progress in US–Iran talks. Lower oil prices hit producers’ realisations and margins, triggering broad declines across upstream names.

items.title: Ola’s 63% IPO crash isn’t the whole story, as 9 have fared worse; 100+ stocks are wealth destroyers
items.description: Despite the steep 63% post-IPO crash in Ola Electric Mobility, it isn’t the worst performer. Over 115 NSE-listed IPOs from 2025–26 now trade below issue price, with several SME listings plunging 70–80%. The broad-based correction underscores deep wealth erosion across sectors amid weak market sentiment.

items.title: Godfrey Phillips, ITC and other cigarette stocks surge up to 12%. What’s driving the rally?
items.description: Shares of ITC Ltd, Godfrey Phillips India Ltd and VST Industries Ltd jumped rose sharply on Wednesday after reports of cigarette makers raising prices to absorb higher excise duties. The move sharply narrows expected EBIT pressure to about 2%, versus earlier forecasts of 8–15%.

items.title: Why Nithin Kamath believes trading with margin funds is a risk without tracking this key metric
items.description: Nithin Kamath warns that margin trading funding is riskier than many realise because traders focus only on interest rates and ignore brokerage charges. He argues that in leveraged trades, every cost—interest, brokerage and taxes—raises the breakeven point, making small price moves far harder to profit from.

items.title: ​FIIs and MFs keep buying these 9 stocks; 3 turn multibaggers, up to 185% rally in FY26
items.description: Large institutional investors often serve as key trendsetters for individual investors, leveraging their in-depth research and analysis to identify promising investment opportunities that might otherwise be overlooked. This influence becomes particularly notable when both foreign institutional investors (FIIs) and mutual funds (MFs) simultaneously increase their stakes in the same stocks.ET Markets’ analysis reveals that major institutional investors, both FIIs and mutual funds, have been steadily increasing their holdings in a select set of companies. Over the past two consecutive quarters (latest being December ’25), both FIIs and MFs raised their stakes in 127 stocks from the BSE 500 list. Nine of these stocks have delivered strong share price gains, rising between 55% and 185% so far in FY26, with three more than doubling investors’ wealth. This consistent buying trend suggests that institutional investors remain optimistic about these companies’ growth prospects. (Data Source: ACE Equity).

items.title: EaseMyTrip shares soar 60% in 3 days. What's driving the rally?
items.description: Shares of Easy Trip Planners surged 60% in three sessions after board approval to raise up to Rs 500 crore. The EaseMyTrip operator plans expansion in hotels and holidays, higher tech investments, and stronger financial flexibility, supported by sequential revenue growth and a return to quarterly profit.

items.title: Market Wrap: Sensex adds 283 points, Nifty tops 25,800 as metal, PSU stocks shine; IT shares keep rally in check
items.description: Benchmark indices ended firm on Wednesday, supported by gains in metals and PSU banks even as IT stocks slipped back into decline. The Sensex rose 283 points and the Nifty added 94. Broader markets advanced, while global cues remained mixed and crude prices edged higher amid U.S.–Iran negotiations.

items.title: Sula Vineyards promoter holding rises to 24.5%, first purchase since IPO in 2022. Check details
items.description: Promoter activity returned at Sula Vineyards as Rajeev Suresh Samant lifted his stake to 24.5%—the first increase since the 2022 IPO.  The move comes despite weak Q3 numbers, with profits hit by tactical destocking in Karnataka and softer demand in Bengaluru.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: E2E Networks, Godfrey Phillips among 7 stocks in spotlight on Wednesday
RITES, Ahluwalia Contracts and 2 smallcap stocks offer up to 30% upside
items.description: Investors looking for fundamental picks can consider these four smallcap stocks which offer up to 30% potential upside. Three of these stocks are from the infra space and Axis Securities recommended them as "conviction buys". Meanwhile, Axis Direct suggested SignatureGlobal India, a realty stock. Here's an account on what could drive these stocks:
